subject: High School Biology
question: Proteins were shown to move about in a plane of the plasma membrane when mouse cellsurface proteins and human cell-surface proteins were observed to integrate along a fused mouse-human cell plasma membrane. Which of the following cell culture techniques was most likely employed in order to yield these results?

options.C: Isolating an immortal variant cell from culture and using it to create a cell line
options.D: Inserting a tumor-inducing virus into a normal cell to initiate transformation

analysis: Step 1: Choice_A: Producing a heterokaryon - This technique involves fusing two cells of different species or types to create a cell with two distinct nuclei. This would allow researchers to observe the integration of proteins from both cells, including mouse cell-surface proteins and human cell-surface proteins, along the fused plasma membrane.

Step 2: Choice_B: Producing a hybrid cell - This technique is similar to producing a heterokaryon, but it involves the fusion of two cells to create a single cell with a single nucleus that contains genetic material from both parent cells. This would also allow researchers to observe the integration of proteins from both cells.

Step 3: Choice_C: Isolating an immortal variant cell from culture and using it to create a cell line - This technique involves selecting a cell that can divide indefinitely and using it to create a cell line. This would not involve the fusion of cells, so it would not allow researchers to observe the integration of proteins from different cells.

Step 4: Choice_D: Inserting a tumor-inducing virus into a normal cell to initiate transformation - This technique involves using a virus to introduce genetic material into a cell, which can cause the cell to become cancerous. This would not involve the fusion of cells, so it would not allow researchers to observe the integration of proteins from different cells.

Step 5: Considering the goal of the experiment, which is to observe the integration of mouse cell-surface proteins and human cell-surface proteins along a fused plasma membrane, the most likely cell culture technique employed would be either producing a heterokaryon or producing a hybrid cell. Both of these techniques involve the fusion of cells, which would allow researchers to observe the integration of proteins from different cells. However, producing a heterokaryon is a more specific technique that involves the fusion of two cells with distinct nuclei, which would provide a clearer view of the integration of proteins from different cells.
